title: Tsaisi

{{Quote|In surrendering this, we surrender ourselves.|Nas Choka relinquishes his tsaisi and surrenders to the Galactic Alliance|<ref name="TUF">''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''</ref>}}
The '''tsaisi''', also called '''baton of rank''' or '''rank baton''', was a smaller, thinner [[Baton/Legends|baton-like]] weapon used by the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Species/Legends|species]] formed out of the same creature as the [[amphistaff]]. Requiring great skill and special training to wield, a tsaisi was reserved only for the most elite [[Officer/Legends|officers]] of the [[warrior caste]], such as the [[Commander/Legends|commander]] [[Shedao Shai]] or [[Warmaster]] [[Tsavong Lah]]. Batons of rank were used through the entire duration of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], culminating with the Yuuzhan Vong surrender, where Warmaster [[Nas Choka]] relinquishing his tsaisi to the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]] forces that bested his people as a symbol of their defeat.

==Description==
[[File:Amphistaff-FF62.png|thumb|left|150px|An amphistaff, the larger and more common cousin of the tsaisi]]
Tsaisi were members of the same<ref name="DT1" /> [[Diet/Legends|predatory]]<ref name="Predators">''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide I: Onslaught]]'' states that tsaisi are smaller members of the same [[Species/Legends|species]] as [[amphistaff]]s, which are shown to be [[Diet/Legends|predators]] in ''[[The New Jedi Order: Traitor]]''.</ref> [[Snake/Legends|serpentine]]<ref name="NJO Sourcebook" /> [[Species/Legends|species]] as the larger and more common [[amphistaff]] and used by Yuuzhan Vong warriors as [[Baton/Legends|baton-like]] [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee weapons]]. Shorter and narrower than its amphistaff cousin, the tsaisi was a delicate<ref name="DT1" /> and precise weapon that required specialized training to be used effectively. Because of the special training and immense skill needed to properly wield a rank baton, they were reserved only for elite warriors and when not in use were worn around the arm as a symbol of rank.<ref name="FF62">{{FactFile|62|YUU 1-2|Yuuzhan Vong Weapons}}</ref> They typically weighed 1.2 [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]]<ref name="NJO Sourcebook" /> and were about the length of a Yuuzhan Vong's [[Limb/Legends|forearm]] when unfurled into a blade.<ref name="DT2" /> In addition to the baton form, tsaisi could also be wielded as a two-headed [[Dagger/Legends|dagger]], [[Whip (weapon)|whip-like]] lash or as a ranged weapon that spit [[Poison/Legends|venom]].<ref name="NJO Sourcebook" />

==History==
Starting with the early warrior-led incurions following the failure of the [[Praetorite Vong]],<ref name="DT1" /> tsaisi were used throughout the entirety of the Yuuzhan Vong [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="TUF" /> [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Shedao Shai]] kept [[Gender/Legends|his]] baton of rank around his right [[Limb/Legends|arm]] and fought with it as he led his forces in a ground [[Battle of Ithor|assault]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ithor/Legends|Ithor]].<ref name="DT2" /> Much later in the war, [[Warmaster]] [[Tsavong Lah]] fought the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] in a [[Battle of Ebaq|duel]] on [[Ebaq 9]] using his own tsaisi and wounded the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Lowbacca]] when he threw the baton like a spear.<ref name="DW">''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]''</ref> Lah's successor as Warmaster, [[Nas Choka]], also bore a tsaisi as a symbol of his rank. After the Yuuzhan Vong were finally [[Battle of Yuuzhan'tar|defeated]] after [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of devastating conflict, Choka publicly relinquished his weapon to the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]] and surrendered on behalf of his people during a ceremony aboard the [[Bothan Assault Cruiser]] ''[[Ralroost]]''.<ref name="TUF" />

==Users==
[[File:TsavongLah3.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Tsavong Lah, who fought Jaina Solo using his tsaisi at Ebaq 9]]
Rank batons were typically used by high ranking members of the Yuuzhan Vong [[warrior caste]], such as the [[Commander/Legends|commander]] [[Shedao Shai]]<ref name="DT1" /> and Warmasters [[Tsavong Lah]]<ref name="DW" /> and [[Nas Choka]].<ref name="FH3">''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic III: Reunion]]''</ref> Even among warriors, a tsaisi was a rare honor<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|266|tsaisi}}</ref> and reserved for only the most elite.<ref name="FF62" /> While the weapons were typically only for warrior [[Officer/Legends|officers]], the [[Shamed Ones|Shamed One]] [[Onimi]] also carried one. This occurrence was so abnormal that [[Nom Anor]],<ref name="Ylesia">''[[The New Jedi Order: Ylesia]]''</ref> a member of the [[intendant caste]],<ref name="AOC1">''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos I: Hero's Trial]]''</ref> was shocked that [[Supreme Overlord]] [[Shimrra Jamaane]] would allow him to carry such a weapon.<ref name="Ylesia" /> In reality, Jamaane was merely a puppet ruler, which accounted for the abnormal allowances given to Onimi, who was secretly the true center of power.<ref name="TUF" />

==Behind the scenes==
Tsaisi first appeared in the [[2000]] novel ''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide I: Onslaught]]'' written by [[Michael A. Stackpole]], the second installment of the ''[[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order]]'' series.<ref name="DT1" /> They appeared in a recurring capacity throughout the rest of the series, until ''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]'', the final book.<ref name="TUF" />
